mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal exhibits no predictive power for earnings revision momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in consumer price inflation and declines in industrial production create stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or uncorrelated with the inflation-supply divergence metric across three consecutive quarters, indicating markets price in accommodative policy or margin resilience.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and consumer credit expansion signals robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet strength, supporting near-term corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when both series rise simultaneously, or equity valuations decouple from demand indicators due to liquidity-driven rallies.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects real economic activity and capacity utilization, positively correlating with near-term equity momentum as investors price in higher current earnings and reduced recession probabilities.
failure_condition: Rank IC between industrial production surprises and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no short-term momentum linkage.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a sustained high-inflation threshold, market participants price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, increasing long-term discount rates and compressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite CPIAUCSL persistently exceeding 240, indicating successful disinflation expectations or dominant liquidity injections overriding rate hike fears.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth coinciding with a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending, driving positive earnings revisions and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns when both series show strength, or wage growth decouples from equity performance due to margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing consumer price inflation signals sustainable economic expansion without overheating, driving positive equity returns over the quarter as corporate earnings grow organically and discount rates remain anchored.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when real GDP growth remains positive and inflation moderates, indicating that valuation multiples contract faster than earnings expand or that policy errors dominate fundamentals.
